mnemonics: DDL DML DCL - Don't Do Lazily, Do Manage Closely (for the three types of database languages)
keyPoints: Data Definition Language (DDL) is used to define and modify the database structure (e.g., CREATE, ALTER, DROP)
Data Manipulation Language (DML) is used for managing data within schema objects (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
The Three-Level Schema Architecture (Internal, Conceptual, External) provides data independence and separates user applications from the physical database
commonMistakes: Confusing DDL with DML commands (e.g., using CREATE when you mean INSERT)
Assuming all users see the same view of the database, overlooking the purpose of external views in the three-level architecture
Thinking that physical data independence means applications are immune to all changes in the database schema
